name: Valhondo Route
name_original: Ermita de San Saturio – running circular por la margen del río Duero
description: Jogging the Ermita de San Saturio trail offers an easy 1.8-mile (2.8 km) circular route along the Río Duero with scenic views.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Pinares traverse a landscape defined by extensive pine forests, offering naturally shaded paths and clean air. The region features diverse terrains, from serene forest trails to more challenging mountain paths, including areas with glacial lagoons and significant peaks. These natural settings provide varied environments for running, catering to different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Pinares The most popular jogging route is Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra, a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers views of the Laguna Negra and the surrounding mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Covaleda Waterfalls Trail, a difficult 4.4 miles (7.0 km) path. This trail leads through forested areas, featuring waterfalls and varied natural scenery. Local runners also love the Valhondo Route, a 1.8 miles (2.8 km) trail leading through pine woodlands, often completed in about 18 minutes. Jogging around Pinares is defined by dense pine forests, glacial lagoons, and varied mountain and riverside paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Pinares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1,100 runners have used komoot to explore Pinares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in the Pinares region?
A: The Pinares region offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 100 trails available. These routes cater to various fitness levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every runner.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Pinares?
A: Yes, Pinares features several excellent circular running routes. For a challenging loop with stunning views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064155512/pico-urbion-loop-from-laguna-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt;. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064184945/embalse-cuerda-del-pozo-running-circular-desde-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic run around the reservoir.

Q: Can I find running trails that feature waterfalls in Pinares?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064189817/covaleda-waterfalls-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Covaleda Waterfalls Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular difficult route that leads through forested areas, showcasing beautiful waterfalls and varied natural scenery. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064168646/mirador-de-castroviejo-cascada-de-la-chorla-y-cueva-serena-trail-running-circular-desde-duruelo-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Castroviejo, cascada de la Chorla y Cueva Serena&lt;/a&gt; for another circular trail featuring a waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural attractions can I expect to see along the running routes in Pinares?
A: Pinares is rich in natural beauty. Many routes offer views of the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6224900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt; and the majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also encounter dense pine forests, glacial lagoons, and diverse mountain and riverside paths. The region's extensive pine forests provide natural shade and clean air, enhancing the running experience.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed run?
A: Yes, Pinares offers several easy routes perfect for a relaxed jog. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064130827/valhondo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valhondo Route&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through serene pine woodlands and typically completed in under 20 minutes. There are 5 easy routes in total, providing gentle options for all.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging trails in Pinares?
A: The running trails in Pinares are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners frequently praise the region's serene pine forests, clean air, and the diverse terrain that caters to different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by significant viewpoints or landmarks?
A: Many routes in Pinares offer stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064155512/pico-urbion-loop-from-laguna-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt; provides exceptional views of the Laguna Negra and the surrounding mountainous landscape. You can also find routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3342444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Cerro de Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Pinares?
A: The extensive pine forests in Pinares provide natural shade, making it comfortable for jogging even during warmer periods. The region's pleasant average annual temperature, often cited between 18 to 22 degrees Celsius in similar pine forest areas, suggests that spring and autumn are particularly ideal, offering mild weather and vibrant scenery. However, the shaded trails make it enjoyable for much of the year.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for more challenging workouts?
A: For those seeking a longer and more challenging run, Pinares has plenty to offer. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064184945/embalse-cuerda-del-pozo-running-circular-desde-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Cuerda del Pozo – running circular desde Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 16.7 km route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064201819/canon-de-casarejos-y-canon-del-rio-lobos-running-circular-desde-casarejos-por-el-barranco-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 14.5 km circular trail.

Q: Can I find running routes along rivers or through canyons in Pinares?
A: Yes, the region offers diverse landscapes including riverside paths and canyons. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14937801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Path Along the Duero&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate 12 km loop from Soria. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064201819/canon-de-casarejos-y-canon-del-rio-lobos-running-circular-desde-casarejos-por-el-barranco-de-la-calzada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de Casarejos y Cañón del río Lobos&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging run through impressive canyon scenery.

Q: Are there any facilities or shelters along the running trails in Pinares?
A: While running, you might come across various facilities. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1838707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Búnker Shelter&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5483774" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente del Berro Shelter&lt;/a&gt; are available in certain areas, providing potential resting spots. Some routes also pass by points like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3950984" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Toledillo Train Stop&lt;/a&gt;, which might offer basic amenities.
tips:
The trail along the Duero River in Soria offers several spectacular routes that combine nature, history, and culture.
Valhondo Route (PR-SO 113)
Route through the Sierra de Santa Ana (PR-SO 112)
Route of the Bridges of the Duero River (Covaleda)
Soria - Garray and the Duero River Banks Route

Tradition has it that in the 6th century the nobleman Saturio from Soria, after the death of his parents, distributed his wealth among the poor and went to live in some caves next to the Duero. There, in a sober and mystical oratory, in honor of Saint Michael the Archangel, he would live for thirty years as a hermit.
Next to the Duero River, we find this beautiful corner, known as Soto Playa, conditioned with bridges and wooden walkways that make the route more fun.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Ermita de San Saturio running route?
faqs.answer: While specific parking lots aren't detailed, the trail is easily accessible along the banks of the Río Duero in Soria. Many visitors park in areas close to the river or the hermitage itself. It's advisable to look for public parking in Soria near the riverfront.

faqs.question: Is the running route along the Río Duero banks dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally considered suitable for outdoor activities, and many natural paths in Spain are dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with other visitors or near wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is beautiful year-round, but it is particularly noted for its stunning scenery in autumn when the landscape along the Duero River is vibrant with color. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit to run this trail. Entry to the Hermitage of San Saturio itself is also free, making it an accessible cultural outing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this running path?
faqs.answer: The trail is considered an "easy hike" with "easily-accessible paths." You can expect a comfortable mix of natural surfaces, likely dirt paths, and some managed sections, including wooden walkways in Soto Playa. It has minimal elevation gain, making it suitable for all fitness levels.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning Riverside Path Along the Duero views. The highlight is undoubtedly the Hermitage of San Saturio, dramatically set into a cliff face overlooking the river. You'll also cross a charming Wooden Footbridge at Soto Playa.

faqs.question: Is this running route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is ideal for beginners. It's classified as "easy" with minimal elevation gain and easily accessible paths, making it a comfortable and calming experience for those new to jogging or looking for a relaxed run.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers 100% of the official trails known as Etapa 03: Soria – Ituero, Lugares Machadianos Soria, and Ruta de Valhondo. It also includes sections of the Sierra de Santa Ana (59%) and Paseo del Príncipe Cautivo (14%).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself is primarily natural, the route is located in Soria, Spain. You can find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the nearby town, especially along the riverfront or in the city center.

faqs.question: What makes the Ermita de San Saturio unique?
faqs.answer: The Hermitage of San Saturio is unique because it's built directly into the natural caves of a cliff overlooking the Duero River. It's a significant historical and religious site, known for its Baroque architecture and intricate frescoes, and is deeply integrated with the natural landscape.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this jogging route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 2.83 km long. For jogging, it typically takes around 19 minutes to complete, depending on your pace. It's a relatively short and enjoyable run.
